title: Intermediaries, Hop-by-Hop Fields, Forwarding, and Transformation · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective intermediary forwarding and transformation contract; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in intermediary forwarding and transformation contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Remove Connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop.
Model downstream and upstream connections, framing, versions, deadlines, addresses, TLS, and failures as independent protocol hops.
Update framing, validators, digests, content codings, warnings, and signatures when an intermediary transforms content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Hop By Hop Removal
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug protocol team handles case 6307 in a partner TLS endpoint negotiating HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 through ALPN. Build deterministic Go evidence to strip dynamically nominated hop fields and repair metadata after transformations; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Remove Connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop.

Inspect the Hop By Hop Removal trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Hop By Hop Removal.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for remove connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Hop By Hop Removal, keep Hop By Hop Removal connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign parse, policy, connect, timeout, upstream, transform, and downstream-write failures to the correct hop and safe response boundary.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Intermediary Failure Ownership, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Every failure observed by a proxy should be reported as an origin status 500.
